title: Warm Terracotta Malaysian Rice Bake
description: A dense, malty rice bake infused with Malaysian spices, perfect for a comforting weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 180C and lightly oil a terracotta baking dish.
Rinse rice until water runs clear, then drain.
In a skillet, heat oil over medium and sauté onion and garlic until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add cumin, coriander, turmeric, lemongrass powder, ginger, and pepper, cooking for another minute.
Stir in rice, coating it with the spice mixture until lightly toasted, about 3 minutes.
Add water, bouillon cubes, salt, and sugar, and bring to a boil.
Transfer mixture to the prepared terracotta dish, cover tightly with foil, and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove foil, stir gently, then cover again and bake an additional 15 minutes until rice is tender and liquid absorbed.
Let the bake sit for 5 minutes before serving.
Garnish with fresh cilantro or toasted coconut flakes if desired, then serve hot.
